title: [ExportVerilog] Omit zext concats on the right hand side of a shift for more readable code
body:
We currently generate things like:

```
wire [3:0] _T_114 = 4'h1 << {2'h0, auto_in_aw_bits_id};
```

But it would be fine (per SV11.6.1) to emit:

```
wire [3:0] _T_114 = 4'h1 << auto_in_aw_bits_id;
```

Because the RHS of a shift has "self-determined" size.  Example IR:

```
    %c0_i20 = hw.constant 0 : i20
    %1 = comb.concat %c0_i20, %auto_in_ar_bits_size : (i20, i3) -> i23
    %2 = comb.shl %0, %1 : i23
```
